get.summary:
Returns a summary row for every payload known to the service.
Optionally filtered by tier.

parameters.name: tierFilter
parameters.description:
Optional tier filter; TIER_UNSPECIFIED means return all tiers.

 - TIER_UNSPECIFIED: Not set; treated as an error in most contexts.
 - TIER_GPU: GPU device memory (fastest, smallest, most volatile).
 - TIER_RAM: Host DRAM / shared-memory segment.
 - TIER_DISK: Local NVMe/SSD/HDD (durable, larger, slower than RAM).
 - TIER_OBJECT: Remote object storage (S3/GCS/Azure; durable, largest).

parameters.name: pageSize
parameters.description: Maximum number of payloads to return. 0 = server default (50); max 500.

parameters.name: pageToken
parameters.description:
Opaque cursor from a previous ListPayloadsResponse.next_page_token.
Omit or leave empty to start from the beginning.

post.summary:
Allocate reserves placement but the payload is invisible to readers
until CommitPayload succeeds.

parameters.name: body
parameters.description:
Allocate reserves placement but payload is not visible
until CommitPayload succeeds.

post.description: BLOCKING policy waits until durable or failure.

delete.description:
If force=true:
- active leases are invalidated immediately
- future reads must fail

post.summary:
Marks payload immutable and readable.
After this point the payload must never change.

delete.description: Idempotent: unpinning an unpinned payload is a no-op.

post.summary: Best-effort advisory that blocks spill while active.
post.description: Pin duration is in milliseconds. duration_ms=0 means indefinite pin until Unpin.

post.summary: Hint to move payload bytes to a faster tier.
Requests movement to a higher tier (RAM/GPU/etc).

child.description:
Canonical payload identifier.

MUST contain exactly 16 bytes (binary RFC4122 UUID).
The server rejects any other length.

This is intentionally NOT a string to avoid formatting ambiguity.

id.description:
Canonical payload identifier.

MUST contain exactly 16 bytes (binary RFC4122 UUID).
The server rejects any other length.

This is intentionally NOT a string to avoid formatting ambiguity.

Append-only history event.

Used for provenance tracking, reproducibility, and lineage context.

PayloadCatalogServicePinBody.description: Stronger than eviction policy but still best-effort.

PayloadCatalogServicePromoteBody.description:
BLOCKING policy waits until requested durability
or definitive failure.

PayloadCatalogServiceUpdatePayloadMetada.description:
Updates the "current" metadata view.

MERGE vs REPLACE behavior is implementation-defined and applies only
to the opaque metadata blob — not placement state.

v1AllocatePayloadRequest.description:
Allocate reserves placement but payload is not visible
until CommitPayload succeeds.

requireDurable.title: Require durable copy before removing from volatile tier

minResidencyTier.title: Attempt to keep payload at or above this tier

v1EvictionPolicy.description:
Advisory eviction hints.

These are not strict guarantees — the system MAY violate them
under critical resource pressure.

v1EvictionPriority.description:
- EVICTION_PRIORITY_UNSPECIFIED: Treated as NORMAL.
 - EVICTION_PRIORITY_LOW: Evict before NORMAL candidates.
 - EVICTION_PRIORITY_NORMAL: Default eviction weight.
 - EVICTION_PRIORITY_HIGH: Evict only after LOW and NORMAL are exhausted.
 - EVICTION_PRIORITY_NEVER: Never automatically evicted (implies no_evict=true).

v1LineageEdge.description:
Descriptive relationships only.
Does NOT enforce execution ordering.

nextPageToken.description: Opaque cursor for the next page. Empty when this is the last page.

totalCount.description: Total number of payloads matching the filter (ignoring pagination).

v1MetadataUpdateMode.description:
Metadata update behavior.
This only affects stored opaque metadata — not placement state.

 - METADATA_UPDATE_MODE_MERGE: Implementation-defined merge semantics
 - METADATA_UPDATE_MODE_REPLACE: Replace entire metadata blob

v1PayloadDescriptor.description:
Authoritative placement descriptor.

version increments whenever:
- tier changes
- location changes
- lifecycle state changes

Clients MUST invalidate cached descriptors when version changes.

v1PayloadID.description:
Canonical payload identifier.

MUST contain exactly 16 bytes (binary RFC4122 UUID).
The server rejects any other length.

This is intentionally NOT a string to avoid formatting ambiguity.

v1PayloadMetadata.description:
Opaque application metadata.

The payload manager stores but NEVER interprets this.
Keep size small (KBs).

v1PayloadState.description:
Lifecycle states are monotonic.
Clients MUST NOT assume a reversible transition.

 - PAYLOAD_STATE_UNSPECIFIED: Not set; treated as an error.
 - PAYLOAD_STATE_ALLOCATED: Reserved; writer has not yet committed data.
 - PAYLOAD_STATE_ACTIVE: Committed and readable by consumers.
 - PAYLOAD_STATE_SPILLING: Being moved to a lower tier; temporarily locked.
 - PAYLOAD_STATE_DURABLE: Confirmed written to a durable tier (DISK/OBJECT).
 - PAYLOAD_STATE_EVICTING: Being removed from its current tier under pressure.
 - PAYLOAD_STATE_DELETING: Deletion in progress.
 - PAYLOAD_STATE_EXPIRED: TTL elapsed; pending final deletion.
 - PAYLOAD_STATE_DELETED: Fully deleted; ID is no longer valid.

v1PromotionPolicy.description:
Promotion policy controls behavior when a payload must move to a higher tier.

 - PROMOTION_POLICY_BEST_EFFORT: Schedule asynchronously if possible
 - PROMOTION_POLICY_BLOCKING: RPC returns only when target tier satisfied or fails

v1SpillPolicy.description:
Spill policy controls durability operations.

 - SPILL_POLICY_BEST_EFFORT: Background durability
 - SPILL_POLICY_BLOCKING: Wait until durable or failure

targetTier.description:
Optional override: spill to this tier instead of the per-payload default.
Must be a tier below the payload's current tier.

v1Tier.description:
Ordered from fastest → most durable.
Higher tiers may not always be available.

 - TIER_UNSPECIFIED: Not set; treated as an error in most contexts.
 - TIER_GPU: GPU device memory (fastest, smallest, most volatile).
 - TIER_RAM: Host DRAM / shared-memory segment.
 - TIER_DISK: Local NVMe/SSD/HDD (durable, larger, slower than RAM).
 - TIER_OBJECT: Remote object storage (S3/GCS/Azure; durable, largest).

id.description:
Canonical payload identifier.

MUST contain exactly 16 bytes (binary RFC4122 UUID).
The server rejects any other length.

This is intentionally NOT a string to avoid formatting ambiguity.

v1LeaseID.description:
Canonical lease identifier.

MUST contain exactly 16 bytes (binary RFC4122 UUID).
The server rejects any other length.

This is intentionally NOT a string to avoid formatting ambiguity.

PayloadStreamServiceAppendBody.description:
============================================================================
APPEND
----------------------------------------------------------------------------
Appends payload UUIDs in order received.

Guarantees:
  - items in request remain ordered
  - requests serialized by server
  - offsets assigned atomically

IMPORTANT:
  Producer must only append AFTER payload commit succeeds.
============================================================================

retentionMaxEntries.title:
retention expressed as logical history
if zero -> infinite retention

offset.title:
assigned by server at append time
never reused, never decreases

payloadId.title: Canonical payload identity returned by PayloadManager::Commit

appendTime.title: when the stream committed the entry

durationNs.title:
logical playback duration of payload
used for paced replay

v1StreamEntry.description:
============================================================================
STREAM ENTRY
----------------------------------------------------------------------------
The fundamental record stored in the commit log.

Ordering rule:
  Offsets are strictly monotonically increasing per stream.
  Offset is the ONLY source of truth for ordering.
  Timestamps must never be used for ordering.

Replay rule:
  Replaying the same offset range must produce identical sequence.

Durability rule:
  Entry is visible only AFTER payload is committed to PayloadManager.
============================================================================
